conbody.p: Impala workload management provides visibility into running and completed queries, which can be accessed through any SQL client.

As queries progress through their lifecycle, their state and relevant metrics are tracked by the coordinator responsible for executing the query. Workload management maintains this data using a dedicated in-memory data structure.

Periodically (according to the defined write interval or max queued settings), the workload management processing loop wakes up to process all the queries within this data structure. The data are batched up into a single insert into DML. This DML is executed resulting in the query lifecycle/metrics data to be inserted into an Apache Iceberg table that is backed by a physical disk store.

At regular intervals—based on the configured write interval or maximum queue settings—the workload management processing loop wakes up to handle the queries in this data structure. The collected data is batched into a single DML INSERT statement, which writes the query lifecycle and metric data into an Apache Iceberg table backed by persistent storage.
Because the data is inserted via a DML statement, at least one active Impala executor must be available in the cluster.

If an error occurs while the DML is executing, the data is re-queued in the in-memory structure and retried during the next processing cycle. Each query's data is attempted up to three times before it is dropped.

Workload management is configured by startup flags on all coordinator processes. The only required flag is --enable_workload_management=true, but other flags provide flexibility to tailor workload management to specific needs. The table below lists the common flags that may need to be modified.

row.entry: Boolean. Enables workload management when set to true
String. Indentifies the current cluster. This value is written to the cluster_id column in the workload management table.
Integer. Number of seconds to wait before batching and inserting completed queries into the query history table. Helps reduce small file creation.

Integer. Maximum number of completed queries that can be queued before being written to the query history table. This limit is independent of query_log_write_interval_s. If the queue reaches this size, the batch is flushed immediately. A value of 0 disables this limit.
Integer. Maximum length of a SQL statement to be recorded in the sys.impala_query_log table. Longer statements are truncated. Escaped characters count toward this limit.
Integer. Maximum length of a query plan to be recorded in the query log. Plans longer than this value are truncated. Escaped characters count toward the limit.
String. Specifies the resource pool used by queries that insert into the sys.impala_query_log table. If unset, no pool is applied.
Integer. Sets the EXEC_TIME_LIMIT_S query option for the DML that inserts records into the sys.impala_query_log table.

conbody.p: Once workload management is enabled, two new tables become available. Both share the same schema.

The first table, sys.impala_query_live, is not a traditional table—it is not backed by files on disk. Instead, it is backed by an in-memory data structure maintained by each Impala coordinator. This table contains all currently running queries, as well as recently completed queries that have not yet been persisted to disk. Data from all coordinators is included in this table. In other words, the table is not limited to queries executed by the coordinator where the query is run.

The second table, sys.impala_query_log, is a regular Apache Iceberg table backed by physical storage. The workload management processing loop on each coordinator periodically moves records from the in-memory structure into this table using INSERT DML statements. Each coordinator inserts only the queries it has overseen.
You can query both tables like any other table in Impala—simply use any SQL client.

Both sys.impala_query_live and sys.impala_query_log share the same schema. The columns in these tables capture detailed metadata about each query, including session information, query options, performance metrics, admission status, memory usage, and more.
The following table describes the columns available in these tables:

row.entry: String. Value of the cluster_id coordinator startup flag.
String. Type of the client session.
String. HiveServer2 protocol version used by the client.
String. Username from the authenticated client.
String. Host and port of the coordinator for the query.
String. Final status of the query.
String. Final state of the query.
String. Final Impala specific state of the query.
String. Type of the query (e.g. QUERY, DDL).
Decimal(18,3). Total time between start and end, in milliseconds.
String. Query options as comma-separated key-value pairs.

entry.codeph: TIMEZONE=America/Los_Angeles,CLIENT_IDENTIFIER=Impala Shell v4.4.0a1 (04bdb4d) built on Thu Mar 27 10:49:35 PST 2025

row.entry: String. Name of the resource pool used.
Bigint. Per-host memory estimate, in bytes.
Bigint. Coordinator memory estimate, in bytes.
String. Comma-separated host and fragment instance count.
Integer. Count of the number of backends used by the query.
String. Admission control result. Not applicable to DDLs.
Integer. Admitted cluster memory in bytes.
String. All considered executor groups (including rejected ones).
Decimal(18,3). Time spent materializing rows, in milliseconds.
Bigint. Bytes spilled to scratch disk.
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Decimal(18,3). Timeline event (milliseconds since the query was received).
Bigint. Total read I/O wait time, in milliseconds.
Bigint. Average read I/O wait time of all executors, in milliseconds.
Bigint. Total bytes read from data cache.
Bigint. Min peak memory usage across nodes.
Bigint. Max peak memory usage across nodes.
Bigint. Mean peak memory usage across nodes.
String. User-provided SQL, redacted by Impala.

entry.codeph: SELECT a.col1, a.col2 from db.tbl a

row.entry: String. Comma-separated string containing all the tables queried in the SQL statement. Aliased tables are resolved to their actual table names.
String. Comma-separated fully qualified table names.
String. Comma-separated list of fully qualified column names from the SQL where clause. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names.
String. Comma-separated list of fully qualified column names from the SQL JOIN clauses. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names.
String. Comma-separated list of fully qualified column names from the SQL GROUP BY and HAVING clauses. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names.
String. Comma-separated list of fully qualified column names from the SQL ORDER BY clause. Aliased columns are resolved to their actual column names.
Bigint. Number of coordinator query slots used.
Bigint. Number of executor query slots used by a single executor.

conbody.p: The following metrics are available to help monitor and understand the behavior of the workload management processing loop in Impala.

ul.li: impala-server.completed-queries.failure - The number of completed queries that failed to be written to the sys.impala_query_log table. If the same query fails to be written multiple times, each attempt increments this counter.

impala-server.completed-queries.max-records-writes - The number of times completed queries were written to the sys.impala_query_log table because the maximum records queued threshold was reached (set by the query_log_max_queued startup flag).

impala-server.completed-queries.queued - The number of completed queries that are currently queued and waiting to be written to the sys.impala_query_log table. Matches the count of rows in sys.impala_query_live.
impala-server.completed-queries.scheduled-writes - The number of times completed queries were written to the sys.impala_query_log table during the regularly scheduled interval.
impala-server.completed-queries.write-durations - A histogram representing the time spent writing completed queries to the sys.impala_query_log table.
impala-server.completed-queries.written - The number of completed queries that were successfully written to the sys.impala_query_log table.

conbody.p: The following log entries help track workload management processing activity.

row.entry: Workload management initialization has started, including create or upgrade of sys.impala_query_live and sys.impala_query_log tables.
Shutdown of workload management has begun. Pending queries will be written to sys.impala_query_log if possible.
Workload management shutdown completed. All pending queries written to sys.impala_query_log.
Shutdown timed out before all queries were written to sys.impala_query_log. The number of lost queries is logged.

entry.codeph: Workload management shutdown timed out. Up to '5' queries may have been lost

row.entry: Failed to run the insert DML for moving completed queries to sys.impala_query_log. The query_id of the failed operation is logged.

entry.codeph: could not write completed query table="sys.impala_query_log" query_id="69477a18fdaadfd7:068f25bb00000000"

row.entry: The insert DML for moving completed queries to sys.impala_query_log exceeded the maximum allowed SQL length.

entry.codeph: Completed queries table insert sql statement of length '9999999999' was longer than the maximum of '2147483647', skipping

row.entry: Successfully inserted completed queries into sys.impala_query_log. Log includes execution stats.

entry.codeph: wrote completed queries table="sys.impala_query_log" record_count=1 bytes="1.11 KB" gather_time="258.382us" exec_time="1s163ms" query_id="9e4a49a89079233f:c72589eb00000000"

row.entry:
Logs data about a failed execution of the workload management insert DML. The error message will also be included in the logs on the next line.
Insert of completed queries to sys.impala_query_log failed. Log includes details.

entry.codeph: failed to write completed queries table="sys.impala_query_log" record_count=1 bytes="1.11 KB" gather_time="258.382us" exec_time="1s163ms" query_id="9e4a49a89079233f:c72589eb00000000"

ul.li: sys.impala_query_live - No maintenance is required. This table resides entirely in memory.

sys.impala_query_log -- As an Iceberg table, it requires periodic maintenance such as computing statistics and expiring old snapshots. Because workloads vary, maintenance is not automated and must be performed manually.
